chunk_summary: Motivational Interviewing (MI) in pediatric nutrition counseling emphasizes partnership, acceptance, compassion, and evocation to elicit change talk and reduce discord within family systems.
learning_objectives: Understand the core principles of Motivational Interviewing (MI) spirit: partnership, acceptance, compassion, and evocation.
Learn to use MI microskills such as open questions, affirmations, reflections, and summaries to elicit change talk.
Apply agenda mapping to prioritize competing concerns in pediatric nutrition counseling.
Utilize DARN-CAT prompts to evoke change talk and support autonomy in decision-making.
key_facts: Motivational Interviewing (MI) is evidence-based and reduces discord while strengthening confidence for change.
MI spirit includes four key elements: partnership, acceptance, compassion, and evocation.
DARN-CAT prompts guide change talk: Desire, Ability, Reasons, Need, Commitment, Action, and Taking steps.
Agenda mapping prevents clinician-patient conflict by prioritizing issues collaboratively (e.g., milk, snacks, sleep).
Open-ended questions, affirmations, reflections, and summaries are essential MI microskills for brief visits.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Maintain the MI spirit: partnership, acceptance, compassion, and evocation, while still acting as a child health advocate when safety thresholds are crossed.
document_key_topics: Change talk elicitation using DARN-CAT prompts
Agenda mapping in pediatric nutrition counseling
